name: MAI-1-preview
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) foundation model, total/active params undisclosed
note: Microsoft AI's first foundation model trained end-to-end in-house (pre+post-trained on ~15,000 H100s); debuted at rank #13 on the LMArena Text leaderboard (per LMArena's own Aug 2025 announcement), the first entry from a new provider into the Top 15. Feeds Copilot text use cases; also offered to trusted testers via API. Context window/param count undisclosed; ctxK is an unconfirmed estimate, not sourced.
verificationNotes:
Confirmed real and released Aug 28 2025 via microsoft.ai/news/two-new-in-house-models (fetched directly): MoE architecture and ~15,000 H100 pre/post-training confirmed on that page. LMArena debut independently confirmed as rank #13 via LMArena's own Aug 2025 announcement (tightens the record's vague '~rank 13-15' to an exact, sourced number). API availability to trusted testers confirmed via independent 2026 coverage. ctxK (128) remains an unverified estimate — no primary source discloses a context window for this model; left as-is per the record's own honest caveat since the schema requires a numeric value. No result rows were present to verify. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): No new benchmark results found for MAI-1-preview (corpus/models/microsoft/mai-1-preview.json) after a widened, methodologically-diversified search. Details:

Vendor check: Microsoft is not a Chinese or Japanese vendor, so the Chinese-language (36Kr/QbitAI/JiQiZhiXin/SuperCLUE/CLUE/OpenCompass) and Japanese-language (note.com/Zenn/Nejumi/Shaberi/Rakuda) search angles specified in the task don't apply here — confirmed via search that Microsoft AI publishes primarily in English.

Sources checked this round (all via live fetch/search, not from memory):
- Artificial Analysis: no MAI-1-preview entry on their Intelligence Index or any evaluation page; only MAI-Transcribe, MAI-Image-2.5, MAI-Voice-1 (different, non-text models) appear.
- OpenRouter: no microsoft/mai-1-preview model page exists; only MAI-Transcribe-1.5, MAI-DS-R1, MAI-Image-2.5, MAI-Code-1-Flash are listed — none is MAI-1-preview.
- Hugging Face: no MAI-1-preview model card (only microsoft/MAI-DS-R1, an unrelated DeepSeek-R1 post-trained variant).
- LMArena / arena.ai leaderboard changelog (official, fetched directly): confirms "MAI-1-preview has been added to the Text Leaderboard" on August 28, 2025 — this matches and corroborates the existing record's rank-#13 claim — but the changelog entry itself carries no numeric Elo score, only the addition date.
- llm-stats.com's live LMArena Text Leaderboard mirror (fetched directly): MAI-1-preview no longer appears at all (as of July 2026 the table shows only current frontier models like Grok-4.1 variants), confirming the model has aged off the live board with no archived numeric score recoverable through this source.
- HuggingFace's lmarena-ai/arena-leaderboard Space and a third-party GitHub Elo-archive repo (oolong-tea-2026/arena-ai-leaderboards) were also checked; neither yielded a retrievable numeric Elo figure for MAI-1-preview's Aug 2025 debut without deeper manual data-file spelunking that a search/fetch pass can't reach, and the GitHub repo is an unofficial third-party aggregator not suitable as a primary citable source.
- Wayback Machine: web.archive.org is not fetchable in this environment (tool error), so historical snapshots of the original microsoft.ai launch page could not be checked directly this round.
- Confirmed (via search) that MAI-1-preview is a distinct, older (Aug 2025) model from Microsoft's newer June-2026 MAI family (MAI-Thinking-1, MAI-Code-1-Flash, etc.) which DO have well-publicized benchmark scores (AIME, SWE-Bench Pro) — care was taken not to conflate MAI-Thinking-1's benchmark numbers with MAI-1-preview, which remains a separate, benchmark-silent model.

Conclusion: MAI-1-preview appears to be a case where Microsoft never published standard quantitative benchmark results (MMLU/GPQA/AIME/etc.) or a citable numeric Arena Elo score — only a qualitative "debuted at #13" leaderboard-rank announcement, which is already captured in the existing record's note field, not as a benchmark result row. No taxonomy-slug-fit result is being forced. This looks like a genuine dead end; further rounds are unlikely to surface new data absent a future re-benchmarking or retrospective publication by Microsoft or a third party. ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): ROUND-8 (2026-07-15): Checked the two specific structured data sources requested, per the task, on top of the prior 7 rounds of general/regional search already documented in corpus/models/microsoft/mai-1-preview.json's verificationNotes. Result: no MAI-1-preview benchmark data found anywhere. Still zero tracked results after 8 rounds.

1. Epoch AI (both feeds fetched and programmatically searched):
   - https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json (179KB, fetched directly) — this endpoint turned out to contain only benchmark *definitions/metadata* (id, title, evaluator, category, description, methodology per benchmark slug like gpqa-diamond, swe-bench-verified, hle, etc.), not model results, so it was not the relevant file for this check.
   - https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv (2.3MB, 5981 rows, fetched directly) — this IS the model x benchmark results file. Parsed with Python csv.DictReader... [truncated; see round13_result.json for full text]

ROUND-14 (2026-07-16): Checked the two specific source types requested this round — HuggingFace model card RAW content and academic citation search (Semantic Scholar / Google Scholar) — on top of all 13 prior rounds already documented above. Result: no new benchmark data found; zero tracked results remains correct.

1. HuggingFace model card check: MAI-1-preview does NOT have a HuggingFace repo. Confirmed via three independent checks: (a) HF's own search API queried directly — `https://huggingface.co/api/models?search=mai-1-preview` (fetched via curl) returned an empty JSON array `[]`, i.e. zero matching repos; (b) `https://huggingface.co/api/models?search=mai-1` returned only unrelated hits (microsoft/MAI-DS-R1 — a DeepSeek-R1 post-trained variant already excluded in prior rounds as a distinct model; plus unrelated repos like maidacundo/open-mythos-140m, maimai11/MAGIC-TTS, VinkuraAI/Maitreyi-Y1 — none is MAI-1-preview or a close variant); (c) WebFetch of https://huggingface.co/microsoft (Microsoft's own org page, 525 total models) listed recently-updated repos (phi-4, UserLM-8b, bitnet-embedding, colipri, HARC variants, etc.) with no MAI-1-preview entry. Since the model has no HF repo at all, there is no model card, no "Evaluation results" metadata table, and no Files-and-versions eval_results.json to check — this source type is a confirmed dead end, not an unexplored gap.

2. Academic citation search: MAI-1-preview does NOT have an indexable arXiv/academic paper, and no third-party paper citing it with benchmark scores was found. Confirmed via: (a) Semantic Scholar Graph API queried directly (`https://api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search?query=MAI-1-preview`, fetched via curl after working around 429 rate-limiting) — returned 186,443 loosely-token-matched results (the API does keyword matching, not exact-phrase), none of which reference Microsoft's MAI-1-preview model; top hits were unrelated papers containing "MAI", "1", or "preview" as separate tokens (Kimina-Prover Preview, TinyR1-32B-Preview, O1-preview replication studies, a C. elegans MAI-1 protein paper, etc.) — zero relevance; (b) WebSearch for `"MAI-1-preview" comparison table arxiv paper cites benchmark score` and `"MAI-1-preview" scholar.google.com` both returned no relevant academic results — no paper was found that names MAI-1-preview in a benchmark comparison table. This confirms MAI-1-preview is a blog-announcement-only model (microsoft.ai/news/two-new-in-house-models) with no accompanying technical report or arXiv paper, so it isn't being cited by third-party NLP papers the way arXiv-published models (e.g. ERNIE 3.0 Titan) are.

Conclusion: both requested source types are genuine, confirmed dead ends for this model (not gaps in search technique) — MAI-1-preview simply has no HF presence and no academic paper trail to mine. Still zero tracked benchmark results after 14 rounds. No result row added this round.

[ROUND 20 — HF-raw-content + academic-citation search, 2026-07-16] MAI-1-preview (corpus/models/microsoft/mai-1-preview.json) still has zero tracked benchmark results after 14 rounds of research. This round (ROUND-14) checked exactly the two source types requested and both are confirmed dead ends, not search gaps:

1. HuggingFace model card: MAI-1-preview has NO HuggingFace repo. Verified directly via HF's search API: `curl https://huggingface.co/api/models?search=mai-1-preview` returned an empty array `[]`. A looser `search=mai-1` query returned only unrelated repos (microsoft/MAI-DS-R1 — a separate DeepSeek-R1 post-trained model already excluded in prior rounds; maidacundo/open-mythos-140m; maimai11/MAGIC-TTS; VinkuraAI/Maitreyi-Y1). A WebFetch of huggingface.co/microsoft (525 models) showed no MAI-1-preview entry among recently-updated repos. With no repo, there is no model card, no rendered 'Evaluation results' table, and no eval_results.json in Files-and-versions to inspect.

2. Academic citation search (Semantic Scholar + Google Scholar style): MAI-1-preview has no arXiv paper or technical report of its own (only a microsoft.ai blog announcement), and no third-party NLP paper was found citing it in a benchmark comparison table. Queried the Semantic Scholar Graph API directly (api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search?query=MAI-1-preview, after working around repeated HTTP 429 rate-limits) — got 186,443 results but all were keyword-token false-positives (papers containing 'MAI', '1', or 'preview' as unrelated substrings, e.g. Kimina-Prover Preview, TinyR1-32B-Preview, O1-preview replication studies, a C. elegans MAI-1 protein biology paper). WebSearch queries for '\"MAI-1-preview\" comparison table arxiv' and '\"MAI-1-preview\" scholar.google.com' both returned no relevant academic hits.

Conclusion: unlike arXiv-published models (e.g. ERNIE 3.0 Titan), MAI-1-preview was never given a technical report/paper, so it has no citation trail to mine, and it was never uploaded to HuggingFace (Microsoft only released it via API to trusted testers plus the LMArena leaderboard debut, both already captured in the existing record). Full trail appended to the model's verificationNotes field in corpus/models/microsoft/mai-1-preview.json.

[ROUND-21 — light delta-check, 2026-07-16] Re-confirmed zero new benchmark data for MAI-1-preview since Round 20 (same date). Checked: (1) WebSearch for "MAI-1-preview" benchmark score 2026 and MMLU/GPQA leaderboard mentions — all hits resolve to MAI-Thinking-1 (the distinct, newer June-2026 reasoning model with AIME/GPQA/SWE-bench Pro scores, already excluded from this record in prior rounds) or generic leaderboard pages with no MAI-1-preview row. (2) Microsoft Learn's "Model benchmarks and leaderboards in Microsoft Foundry" page (learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/concepts/model-benchmarks, a source type not previously checked) fetched directly — describes Foundry's quality-index methodology (MMLU-Pro, GPQA, MATH, LiveCodeBench, etc.) but is a methodology doc, not a per-model results table. (3) Direct fetch of ai.azure.com/catalog/models/MAI-1-preview — the model's own Foundry catalog page returns "No Data Available: We couldn't find any data to display here," confirming MAI-1-preview was never onboarded into the Foundry model leaderboard (consistent with it being a trusted-tester-API/LMArena-only release, never sold via Azure). (4) Re-verified the LMArena #13 debut via lmarena_ai's own Aug 2025 X/Twitter announcement (x.com/lmarena_ai/status/1961112908026593557) — reconfirms the rank-only claim already in this record's note field, still no numeric Elo. No new evidence found; zero tracked benchmark results remains the correct state for this model.
